title: rubus viridulus
intro: Rubus viridulus, also known as the green bramble or green raspberry, is a plant species in the rose family that is native to Eastern Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its rich content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rubus viridulus include its ability to aid in digestion, lower blood pressure, and reduce the risk of certain cancers, as well as its potential to improve cardiovascular health and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rubus viridulus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves, stems, and roots of the plant in hot water or a solvent.
